item.title: Sounds like smart one
item.content:encoded: Well anyone buying cryptocurrency pretty much. Wait. Dollars get tossed for being vector for contagion, just waiting really. Then I'm sure it will fall to 0 as usual. Or, one could get ahead of it idea wise. Guide the chaos. Controlled chaos only gets so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know nothing about economy and 4333 wouldn't care, but wouldn't the ability to generate infinite amounts of money and spending it extravagantly cause more inflation in the global (dude has fake IDs everywhere) economy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the best SCPs I have ever read.

It not only shows the flaws of the Foundation, but also makes you question whether the SCP is either a good or a bad person. The dialogue is entertaining and the insults, whoo that's some good ones he's (and you too, author) got there.

Overall, a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great article. Quite underrated for everything here: the story, the amount of thought that went into this, the dialogue, hell, even the sheer length of this thing is impressive. It's brilliant how far you took this relatively simple concept.
Note: Just edited the item number in the ACS header from 3396 to 4333 (hope that's okay with you). I'm surprised this went undetected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This basic main idea works great as simplistic absurdism and social commentary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew you wrote this when the “corpse in advanced decomposition” came up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this guy. Real "end boss villain" sort of feel about him.

This one seems rather overlooked, which is a shame. I think it's very good. Mostly carried by the central character, but still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this, but I wish there was a bit more to it, though. Efforts should be made to understand what it is, and the interviewer didn't use 4333 to find any more related anomalies. No vote for now

item.title: Staff Post- Closed
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I don't know what this shit is, but it stops now.

This line of discussion, such as it is, ends here.

item.title: Clarification
item.content:encoded:
Nobody said anything about letting her die. The reason that only Americans use this trope is that Americans live in a country without universal healthcare. And that's all I meant in the first place.

We cool?

item.title: Re: dolla dolla bills, y'all
item.content:encoded:
only a coldhearted bastard would let their niece die in the name of a job.

we all arrange the foundation differently, bud. and we all have different values. you can take your salt somewhere else, i ain't need it. vote accordingly ♥

item.title: Re: dolla dolla bills, y'all
item.content:encoded:
It's obvious you're American.

Only an American would write about someone betraying everything he's ever believed in so he can pay for someone else's health insurance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was entertaining as it was educational. Your writing is top notch; I read the whole thing interested, and despite its length, it never lagged.

+ show block
– hide block

Honestly, it's enough to give a man a cardiac episode, if I had a cardiac capable of being episodic.

I forgot to suggest that “cardiac” being an adjective here is awkwardly installed in the sentence. Maybe it’s character-speak and you are going for that but “cardiovascular system” or even “heart”would be more appropriate.

item.title: dolla dolla bills, y'all
item.content:encoded:
a man for whom money can buy happiness. and pretty much everything else.

many thanks to the folks in chat that helped me work this one through. it's ill-advised and messy despite your help, but in the end i guess i just can't help myself.

and thank you, readers, for investing wisely. ♥
